## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about adding an exit pop over to your registration page giving them the opportunity to receive something cool as they leave if they don't register for your webinar and of course they have to opt in to get it so the theory here is The group of people the 70% who aren't going to register for a webinar many of them will never even Click on something to come to a webinar registration, but some of them will and of those who come Who say I'm not registering for that webinar as they leave you give them some incentive? For opting in so you captured their email address in that process so Keys to success. How do you make this work first off and I've said this over and over again I hope you've gotten the message the opt-in incentive that you offer has to be something they really want and It should be something different than the webinar registration bonus. So the webinar registration bonus might be this Report or this video or this whatever that gift is you need to give a different thing for this opt-in incentive Third it needs to only be shown on exit, which is actually a technical term in the pop over space So this it means it shows up when someone goes up to hit the back button Or when they go up to put some other URL in the navigation bar in their browser And when they do that it then pops this up so that they get the option to opt in also and Ideally well not ideally You failed if they see this offer if they register for the webinar and It should be something that moves them towards the mindset that they need to achieve in Order to buy just like everything else in this funnel This can be a major contributor also and it doesn't offer your product that'll come later It doesn't even talk about your product yet But move them towards The mindset they need in order to be able to buy Now to make this work You actually need two separate versions of your webinar registration page now This assumes that you're gonna run paid advertising to your webinar registration page I Put this this way because I just assume you're gonna do that because it's such a good idea and so if you're not going to run a Advertising to your page then you can ignore this thing about having two separate versions But it's something that I just assume you're a smart person You're gonna want to do anyway So I'm assuming you're going to create two different versions one with an exit pop-up which will be shown to all your non ad traffic and One without an exit pop-up because you can't let exit pop-ups on traffic that come from ads It's against the rules of Facebook and Google and everybody else

## [03:02 - 06:05]

And so you don't want to have a pop-up on that page So here's how it works The traffic that comes from emails that you send out is going to go to the webinar registration page with the exit pop-up The traffic that comes from ads is going to go to the second version of your page which has no pop-up Then the external traffic the ones where you don't have their email address that are not ad-based traffic Are going to go to the one with the pop-up Now you've already seen this slide before But just to remind you the types of external traffic to show the pop-ups to or social media traffic So the stuff that you just naturally are posting on Facebook and and all the other different sites out there Podcast traffic natural traffic that comes to your site Physical marketing your business cards your flyers your signage affiliate traffic basically any source of People that come to your page other than from your email list or ads But Don you ask why should I show this pop-up and ask people to re-opt in again if they're already on my list Well, that's good question But we still do it because that extra opt-in Shows that they're interested in this particular topic It's almost the equivalent of those of you who have studied Jeff Walker's product launch formula He talks about the launch list about taking the people who are on your main list and Get them to opt-in again for another list that says that they're going to get promotions for this launch That's exactly what's happening here. It's showing you and it's getting a micro commitment from them That will move them closer to purchasing later on Also while we're on that subject the content of your gift moves them towards purchasing later on and sometimes that gift will actually Get people to register for the webinar and so there's lots of good things that happen by doing this And so I do suggest you do this even for traffic that is coming from Emails that you send out to them All right, so how do you show the exit pop-up your web page building program probably has this technology already in place And as we mentioned elsewhere if it doesn't you may want to change them because there are a dime a dozen There's a bunch of different ones out there. They're about equal in Capabilities and so hey you may want to just switch to one that does and most marketing based WordPress themes have it like thrive themes Has it built in and so you don't have to buy another program to do this if you don't have This capability and either one of those then you may need to actually go search for and buy it Program that allows you to be able to put pop-ups on your page There are a dime a dozen to there's bunch of them out there. You shouldn't pay more than 30 bucks for one

## [06:05 - 07:07]

and then Whatever you're using just search for their instructions on how to run exit pop-ups and it'll show you how to do it All right, so what do you do once someone opts in so you run an email Series that delivers that thing that you promised them and that prepares them for the rest of the webinar funnel Because even though they didn't sign up for the webinar itself You are going to bring them back into the webinar funnel at the webinar replay stage And so you want this Series to happen in two to three days so that they're done with it before you're coming back in with your replay promo stage and then make sure just To restate this make sure you test this so you make sure it doesn't appear when someone registers for the webinar it shouldn't But just to make sure Test it. You don't want to cause confusion
